title: pow: mine_token has no timeout; blocks spawn_blocking thread at max difficulty
description: mine_token() in pow.rs iterates nonces in 0u64.. with no bound or cancellation point. At diff=30 (MAX_DIFFICULTY), the expected iteration count is 2^30 ≈ 10^9. On typical hardware (100M double-SHA256/s) this takes ~10 seconds of CPU time. The call is wrapped in spawn_blocking in transport.rs, which correctly offloads it from the async executor. However, spawn_blocking threads are limited (default 512 in tokio); if many concurrent auth handshakes are in flight, all threads can be saturated for the duration of mining. There is no way to cancel a mining task if the connection drops mid-handshake. Consider adding an abort channel parameter or a maximum iteration count that returns Err if exceeded.
